assertions.title: Protected routes redirect guests to login with destination preserved

steps.action: Open /dashboard as a signed-out guest in the assigned browser session.
steps.expected: The app redirects to /login with return_to preserving /dashboard.
steps.observed: The browser landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/login?return_to=%2Fdashboard and rendered the login surface.

steps.expected: The redirect keeps the intended protected destination including the query string.
The app redirects to /login with return_to preserving /onboarding.

evidence.network: Browser navigation showed guest protected-entry redirects to /login?return_to=... for /dashboard, /dashboard?tab=settings, and /onboarding. The request log captured protected document requests such as GET /dashboard?tab=settings and GET /onboarding followed by GET /login?return_to=%2Fdashboard%3Ftab%3Dsettings and GET /login?return_to=%2Fonboarding, plus GET http://api.faworra.localhost:1355/api/auth/get-session as the guest bootstrap request.

steps.action: Open /login?return_to=https%3A%2F%2Fevil.example as a guest and sign in with the assigned ready user.
steps.expected: Successful sign-in stays on the app origin and falls back to a safe in-app destination.
steps.observed: After sign-in the browser landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard and did not navigate to the external origin.
After sign-in the browser again landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard.

steps.expected: The encoded slash-backslash variant does not escape the app.
steps.observed: After sign-in the browser again landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard.

evidence.network: Each unsafe login URL kept the browser on the dashboard.faworra.localhost origin and resolved to /dashboard after successful sign-in. The better-auth.session_token cookie was present after sign-in. In this environment agent-browser did not surface the intermediate sign-in form POST in its request log, so the safe-final-URL and authenticated cookie state were used as supporting network evidence.

assertions.title: Authenticated sessions are redirected away from login appropriately

steps.action: While authenticated as the assigned ready user, open /login.
steps.expected: The user cannot remain on the public login page and is sent to the protected dashboard.
steps.observed: Opening /login immediately landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard with the authenticated dashboard shell visible.

evidence.network: GET /login in an authenticated ready-user session resolved to the protected dashboard URL without any observed 5xx render failure.

assertions.title: Sign-out returns to the public surface and restores route protection

steps.action: From the dashboard, open the user menu for the assigned ready user.
steps.expected: Signed-in UI state is visible before sign-out.
steps.observed: The dashboard showed the Auth Shell ready user menu button and its Sign Out menu action.

steps.action: Use the dashboard Sign Out action.
steps.expected: The app returns to a usable signed-out auth surface.
steps.observed: The browser landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/login and rendered the signed-out auth form.

steps.expected: The protected route redirects back to login again.
steps.observed: The browser landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/login?return_to=%2Fdashboard and no auth cookie remained in the session.

evidence.network: The dashboard Sign Out action returned the browser to /login, and a follow-up visit to GET /dashboard redirected to /login?return_to=%2Fdashboard. The browser cookie jar was empty afterward, confirming the signed-out session state.

assertions.title: Invalid or stale session cookies recover to a usable login surface

steps.action: Open the login surface in a fresh assigned browser session and inject better-auth.session_token=invalid-stale-token for the dashboard origin.
steps.expected: The browser holds an invalid auth cookie before testing protected-route recovery.
steps.observed: The session cookie jar showed better-auth.session_token=invalid-stale-token before visiting a protected route.

steps.action: With the invalid cookie still present, open /dashboard.
steps.expected: The app recovers to a usable login surface without an infinite redirect loop.
steps.observed: The browser landed on http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/login?return_to=%2Fdashboard, settled normally, and showed actionable Email/Password login controls without a redirect loop.

evidence.network: With better-auth.session_token manually set to an invalid value, protected-route entry recovered to /login?return_to=%2Fdashboard and stabilized on the signed-out auth surface; no looping navigation was observed during load settlement.
evidence.cookieState: better-auth.session_token remained set to the invalid value, but the app treated the session as unauthenticated and rendered the usable login surface.

assertions.title: Existing-user protected entry resumes after sign-in and readiness

steps.expected: The app redirects to /login with the intended destination preserved in return_to.

steps.action: From that login redirect, sign in with the existing ready fixture user.
steps.expected: The app resumes the originally requested protected destination after authentication.
steps.observed: After sign-in the browser resumed http://dashboard.faworra.localhost:1355/dashboard?tab=settings instead of leaving the user on /login or dropping the intended destination.

evidence.network: The guest protected-entry navigation landed on /login?return_to=%2Fdashboard%3Ftab%3Dsettings, and successful sign-in resumed the exact protected URL /dashboard?tab=settings on the same app origin.

frictions.description: agent-browser's request log did not reliably surface the intermediate sign-in form POSTs after successful login, even though the redirect/document requests and resulting auth cookie state were observable.
frictions.resolution: Used the browser's final URL, rendered protected destination, and cookie presence/absence as supporting auth-network evidence for the login and sign-out assertions.

summary: Tested 6 browser assertions for auth-shell-parity: 6 passed, 0 failed, 0 blocked. Guest protected-entry redirects preserved destinations, unsafe return_to values were neutralized, authenticated /login redirected to the dashboard, protected-entry sign-in resumed the intended route, sign-out restored signed-out protection, and invalid session-cookie recovery returned to a usable login surface without a redirect loop.
